key_entities: exit codes 0 merged/1 needs_rebase/2 ci_failed/3 review_failed/4 not_requested/5 abandoned/6 lander_down/7 error/75 pending/124 timed_out
propositions: A project-tooling command collapses agent monitoring into a single call returning one typed terminal outcome with a distinct exit code per outcome.
land --wait checks the bot heartbeat first and only sets the Autosubmit label under the agent's own Gerrit identity when the heartbeat is fresh, avoiding orphaned labels.
A stale (older than 90s) or unreachable status endpoint returns lander_down immediately rather than hanging to the 30-min timeout, treating unconfirmed liveness conservatively.

The outcome is derived by fallback precedence: the bot's recorded outcome (S3) wins, else native Gerrit state maps to ci_failed/review_failed/merged/abandoned, with needs_rebase only obtainable from the S3 record.
land-status is a single-shot variant returning pending while the bot drives and not_requested on a change with no bot record or Autosubmit set.
Gerrit REST access reuses the repo's stdlib-only urllib+Basic-auth+XSSI client pattern with the outcome enum documented as a versioned contract in docs/land-contract.md.
